<result>Here is the complete implementation plan for the `aws-s3` skill.

---

## Implementation Plan: aws-s3 Skill (PAN-53)

### 1. Package Name Decision

Use **`aws-s3`** as the skill directory and component name. This follows the existing pattern of hyphenated, service-oriented names (`google-workspace`, `zoho-mail`). It will be published to clawhub as `panthrocorp-aws-s3`. The npm package name in `package.json` should be `@panthrocorp/aws-s3` -- this is what consumers will `require()`.

### 2. Directory Structure

```
aws-s3/
  package.json          # npm package manifest (name, version, main, scripts, devDependencies)
  src/
    index.mjs           # Source entry point: re-exports from @aws-sdk/client-s3
  esbuild.config.mjs    # esbuild bundler configuration
  dist/                  # (gitignored) build output
    index.cjs            # Bundled CommonJS output
  test/
    exports.test.mjs     # Verifies expected symbols are exported from the bundle
  SKILL.md              # clawhub manifest
  CHANGELOG.md          # release-please managed
  LICENSE               # MIT-0 (copy from repo root)
  CLAUDE.md             # Agent context file
  README.md             # Developer documentation
  .npmignore            # Ensures only dist/ + package.json + SKILL.md + LICENSE ship in tarball
```

No `.goreleaser.yml` -- that is Go-specific. The build/release flow uses `npm run build` and `npm pack` instead.

### 3. package.json

```json
{
  "name": "@panthrocorp/aws-s3",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents",
  "main": "dist/index.cjs",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist/",
    "SKILL.md",
    "LICENSE"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "node esbuild.config.mjs",
    "test": "node --test test/exports.test.mjs",
    "prepack": "npm run build"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@aws-sdk/client-s3": "^3.750.0",
    "esbuild": "^0.25.0"
  },
  "license": "MIT-0",
  "author": "panthrocorp",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills",
    "directory": "aws-s3"
  }
}
```

Key decisions:
- `@aws-sdk/client-s3` is a **devDependency** because it gets bundled into `dist/index.cjs` at build time. The published tarball has zero runtime dependencies.
- `main` points to `dist/index.cjs` (CommonJS) because the gateway container uses `require()`.
- The `files` array controls what `npm pack` includes. No `.npmignore` needed if `files` is specified (they are mutually exclusive; `files` is a whitelist and is cleaner).
- `node --test` is the built-in Node.js test runner (available since Node 18), avoiding a test framework dependency.

### 4. esbuild Configuration (esbuild.config.mjs)

```js
import { build } from "esbuild";

await build({
  entryPoints: ["src/index.mjs"],
  bundle: true,
  platform: "node",
  target: "node18",
  format: "cjs",
  outfile: "dist/index.cjs",
  minify: false,        // Keep readable for debugging in gateway
  sourcemap: false,     // Not needed for SDK re-export
  legalComments: "eof", // Preserve license comments at end of file
});
```

Key decisions:
- `platform: "node"` tells esbuild to treat Node built-ins (`http`, `https`, `stream`, etc.) as external -- they are not bundled. This is critical because the AWS SDK uses them.
- `format: "cjs"` because the gateway uses `require()`.
- `target: "node18"` -- conservative minimum, matching typical container base images.
- `minify: false` -- the bundle is not shipped to browsers; readability in the container aids debugging. The size difference is negligible for a server-side package.

### 5. Source Entry Point (src/index.mjs)

```js
export {
  S3Client,
  ListObjectsV2Command,
  GetObjectCommand,
  PutObjectCommand,
  DeleteObjectCommand,
  HeadObjectCommand,
  ListBucketsCommand,
  CopyObjectCommand,
  CreateMultipartUploadCommand,
  UploadPartCommand,
  CompleteMultipartUploadCommand,
  AbortMultipartUploadCommand,
  GetBucketLocationCommand,
} from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";
```

This re-exports all commonly needed symbols. The Linear ticket calls out `S3Client`, `ListObjectsV2Command`, and `GetObjectCommand` as immediate requirements, but including the other common commands costs nothing (they are all in the same package) and avoids a breaking change when the next consumer needs `PutObjectCommand`.

Alternatively, the simplest approach is a full barrel re-export:

```js
export * from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";
```

This is cleaner and future-proof, though the bundle will be slightly larger. Recommend the wildcard re-export for v1 since the size difference is minimal for a server-side package.

### 6. SKILL.md

```yaml
---
name: AWS S3
description: Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents
version: 0.1.0
author: panthrocorp
license: MIT-0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    requires:
      npm:
        - "@panthrocorp/aws-s3"
    emoji: "\U0001FAA3"
    homepage: https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills
    os: ["linux"]
---

# AWS S3 Skill

Pre-bundled AWS S3 SDK for use inside the OpenClaw gateway container. All transitive
dependencies are bundled -- no internet access required at install time.

## Installation

Install from the release tarball into the gateway container:

\```bash
TAG=$(curl -fsSL "https://api.github.com/repos/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases" \
  | grep -o '"tag_name":"aws-s3/v[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
VERSION=${TAG#aws-s3/v}
URL="https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases/download/${TAG}/panthrocorp-aws-s3-${VERSION}.tgz"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install "$URL" --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
\```

Or from a downloaded tarball:

\```bash
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install /path/to/panthrocorp-aws-s3-0.1.0.tgz --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
\```

## Usage

\```js
const { S3Client, ListObjectsV2Command, GetObjectCommand } = require("@panthrocorp/aws-s3");

const client = new S3Client({});  // uses default credential provider chain (IMDS)

const list = await client.send(new ListObjectsV2Command({ Bucket: "my-bucket" }));
const obj = await client.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: "my-bucket", Key: "file.txt" }));
\```

## Authentication

Credentials are resolved via the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. In an EC2/ECS
environment, this uses IMDS (Instance Metadata Service) automatically. No environment
variables or credential files are required.

## Important

- This package bundles @aws-sdk/client-s3 with all transitive dependencies.
- No network access is needed at install time.
- The gateway container must have Node.js available.
```

Notable differences from the Go skills' SKILL.md:
- `requires.npm` instead of `requires.bins` -- this is a new metadata key indicating the skill is an npm package rather than a binary.
- No `requires.env` -- credentials come from IMDS, not environment variables.
- The installation section uses `npm install` with a tarball URL instead of `tar -xz`.

**Gotcha**: The `requires.npm` field is new and may not be recognized by existing clawhub/openclaw tooling. This should be validated with the clawhub team. If `requires.npm` is not supported, use a `requires.files` or similar field, or document the install command in the markdown body only and omit machine-readable npm metadata for now.

### 7. Test Strategy (test/exports.test.mjs)

```js
import { describe, it } from "node:test";
import { strict as assert } from "node:assert";

describe("aws-s3 bundle exports", () => {
  it("exports S3Client", async () => {
    const mod = await import("../dist/index.cjs");
    assert.equal(typeof mod.S3Client, "function");
  });

  it("exports ListObjectsV2Command", async () => {
    const mod = await import("../dist/index.cjs");
    assert.equal(typeof mod.ListObjectsV2Command, "function");
  });

  it("exports GetObjectCommand", async () => {
    const mod = await import("../dist/index.cjs");
    assert.equal(typeof mod.GetObjectCommand, "function");
  });

  it("S3Client can be instantiated", async () => {
    const mod = await import("../dist/index.cjs");
    // Should not throw -- does not make network calls until .send()
    const client = new mod.S3Client({ region: "us-east-1" });
    assert.ok(client);
    client.destroy();
  });
});
```

Tests run against the **built** bundle (`dist/index.cjs`), not the source. This validates that esbuild produced a working artifact. The `npm test` script requires `npm run build` first; CI will run them sequentially.

### 8. CI Changes (.github/workflows/ci.yml)

Add a new job `build-npm` alongside the existing Go `build` job. Update `gate` to depend on both.

New job to add:

```yaml
  build-npm:
    name: Build & Test (npm)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: aws-s3
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"

      - name: Check for skill changes
        id: changes
        run: |
          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
          PATTERN='^aws-s3/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
          if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
            echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Install dependencies
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm run build

      - name: Test
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm test

      - name: Pack (dry-run)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm pack --dry-run

      - name: Skip (no skill changes)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
        run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"
```

Update the `gate` job:

```yaml
  gate:
    name: gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build, build-npm]
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Check build result
        run: |
          for result in "${{ needs.build.result }}" "${{ needs.build-npm.result }}"; do
            if [ "$result" = "failure" ] || [ "$result" = "cancelled" ]; then
              echo "A build failed or was cancelled"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
          echo "All checks passed"
```

### 9. Release Pipeline Changes

#### .release-please-config.json

Add a new entry in `packages`:

```json
"aws-s3": {
  "release-type": "node",
  "component": "aws-s3",
  "changelog-path": "CHANGELOG.md",
  "extra-files": [
    {
      "type": "yaml",
      "path": "SKILL.md",
      "jsonpath": "$.version"
    }
  ]
}
```

`release-type: "node"` tells release-please to read/bump the version from `package.json` (not a Go module). It will also update `package.json` version on release PRs and update `package-lock.json` if present.

#### .release-please-manifest.json

Add the initial version:

```json
{
  "google-workspace": "0.5.0",
  "zoho-mail": "0.3.0",
  "aws-s3": "0.1.0"
}
```

#### .github/workflows/release-please.yml

Add outputs and a new publish job.

New outputs in the `release-please` job:

```yaml
      s3_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--release_created'] }}
      s3_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--tag_name'] }}
```

New publish job:

```yaml
  publish-aws-s3:
    name: Publish aws-s3
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.s3_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: aws-s3
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Test before release
        run: npm run build && npm test

      - name: Build and pack
        id: pack
        run: |
          npm run build
          TARBALL=$(npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename')
          echo "tarball=$TARBALL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Upload tarball to GitHub release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${TAG#aws-s3/v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
```

Key differences from Go publish jobs:
- No GoReleaser step. Replaced by `npm run build && npm pack`.
- No local version tag creation (Go skills need this because GoReleaser expects a simple `vX.Y.Z` tag). The release-please tag `aws-s3/v0.1.0` is sufficient.
- The release artifact is a single `.tgz` tarball (output of `npm pack`) instead of multiple platform-specific `tar.gz` files.
- `npm pack --json` returns structured JSON with the filename, making it easy to capture the tarball name.

### 10. Dependabot Changes (.github/dependabot.yml)

Add an npm ecosystem entry:

```yaml
  - package-ecosystem: npm
    directory: /aws-s3
    schedule:
      interval: weekly
    commit-message:
      prefix: "fix"
    labels:
      - dependencies
    open-pull-requests-limit: 5
```

### 11. .gitignore

Add an `aws-s3/.gitignore` or add to a root `.gitignore`:

```
aws-s3/node_modules/
aws-s3/dist/
```

The `dist/` directory is a build artifact and should not be committed. It is regenerated by `npm run build` in CI and during `npm pack` (via the `prepack` script).

### 12. CLAUDE.md for aws-s3

```markdown
# aws-s3

## Overview

Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents. Unlike the Go-based skills,
this is an npm package that bundles @aws-sdk/client-s3 with all transitive dependencies
into a single CommonJS file.

## Architecture

- `src/index.mjs` -- barrel re-export of @aws-sdk/client-s3 symbols
- `esbuild.config.mjs` -- bundles src/index.mjs into dist/index.cjs (CommonJS, Node 18+)
- `dist/index.cjs` -- (built, gitignored) the complete bundled SDK
- `test/exports.test.mjs` -- validates the bundle exports expected symbols

## Build

npm ci && npm run build

## Test

npm test  (requires build first)

## How it works

The gateway container has Node.js but no internet and no @aws-sdk installed.
This package bundles @aws-sdk/client-s3 and all its transitive dependencies
into a single CJS file using esbuild. The tarball from `npm pack` is installed
into the container via `npm install <tarball>`.

## Credentials

Uses the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. On EC2/ECS this resolves
via IMDS automatically. No environment variables are required.
```

### 13. Gotchas and Edge Cases

1. **`requires.npm` in SKILL.md is a new convention.** The existing skills use `requires.bins`. Confirm with the clawhub/openclaw team that `requires.npm` is a recognized field, or that unrecognized fields are ignored gracefully. If not supported, omit the machine-readable field and rely on the markdown install instructions.

2. **esbuild and Node built-ins.** With `platform: "node"`, esbuild marks Node built-ins as external. This is correct. However, if `@aws-sdk/client-s3` has any optional native dependencies (it should not, but verify), those would fail to bundle. The AWS SDK v3 is pure JavaScript, so this should not be an issue.

3. **Bundle size.** `@aws-sdk/client-s3` v3 is modular, but the full client with credential providers pulls in a significant dependency tree. Expect the bundle to be 1-3 MB. This is acceptable for a server-side package installed once into a container.

4. **`npm pack` tarball naming.** The default name is `panthrocorp-aws-s3-0.1.0.tgz` (npm lowercases and replaces `@`/`/` with the org prefix pattern). The `npm pack --json` approach in the release workflow handles this dynamically.

5. **`npm ci` requires `package-lock.json`.** The `aws-s3/` directory must commit a `package-lock.json`. Without it, `npm ci` fails. Generate it by running `npm install` during initial setup and commit it.

6. **release-please `node` release-type.** This release type bumps `version` in `package.json` and `package-lock.json` automatically. The `extra-files` YAML updater for `SKILL.md` handles the manifest version separately. Both will be updated in the release PR.

7. **No `prepack` in CI.** The `prepack` script in `package.json` runs `npm run build` before `npm pack`. In the release workflow, `npm run build` is called explicitly before `npm pack`, so the build runs twice. This is harmless (idempotent) but could be optimized by removing the explicit build call and relying on `prepack`. However, keeping the explicit call is clearer for CI readability.

8. **Credential provider bundling.** The default credential provider chain (`@aws-sdk/credential-provider-node`) is pulled in transitively by `S3Client`. This includes IMDS, ECS container credentials, environment variables, and shared credentials file providers. All of these are pure JS and will bundle correctly. Verify after the first build that instantiating `new S3Client({})` does not throw import errors.

9. **Container Node.js version.** The esbuild `target: "node18"` should match or be lower than the gateway container's Node version. If the gateway runs Node 20+, `node18` is safe. If it runs Node 16, adjust the target.

10. **Renaming the existing Go `build` job.** For clarity, consider renaming it to `build-go` when adding `build-npm`. This is optional but improves readability. The `gate` job's `needs` array would change to `[build-go, build-npm]`.

### 14. Implementation Sequence

1. Create `aws-s3/` directory with `package.json`, `src/index.mjs`, `esbuild.config.mjs`, `test/exports.test.mjs`
2. Run `npm install` to generate `package-lock.json`
3. Run `npm run build` and `npm test` locally to validate
4. Add `SKILL.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `LICENSE`, `README.md`, `.gitignore`
5. Update `.release-please-config.json` (add `aws-s3` package entry)
6. Update `.release-please-manifest.json` (add `"aws-s3": "0.1.0"`)
7. Update `.github/dependabot.yml` (add npm entry)
8. Update `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (add `build-npm` job, update `gate`)
9. Update `.github/workflows/release-please.yml` (add outputs + `publish-aws-s3` job)
10. Update root `CLAUDE.md` to mention npm-based skills as a second pattern
11. Open PR, verify CI passes (the `build-npm` job should detect changes and run)

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/.release-please-config.json`
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/.github/workflows/release-please.yml`
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/.github/dependabot.yml`
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/google-workspace/SKILL.md` (reference for SKILL.md format)</result>

## Implementation Plan: aws-s3 Skill (PAN-53)

### 1. Package Name Decision

Use **`aws-s3`** as the skill directory and component name. This follows the existing pattern of hyphenated, service-oriented names (`google-workspace`, `zoho-mail`). It will be published to clawhub as `panthrocorp-aws-s3`. The npm package name in `package.json` should be `@panthrocorp/aws-s3` -- this is what consumers will `require()`.

### 2. Directory Structure

```
aws-s3/
  package.json          # npm package manifest (name, version, main, scripts, devDependencies)
  src/
    index.mjs           # Source entry point: re-exports from @aws-sdk/client-s3
  esbuild.config.mjs    # esbuild bundler configuration
  dist/                  # (gitignored) build output
    index.cjs            # Bundled CommonJS output
  test/
    exports.test.mjs     # Verifies expected symbols are exported from the bundle
  SKILL.md              # clawhub manifest
  CHANGELOG.md          # release-please managed
  LICENSE               # MIT-0 (copy from repo root)
  CLAUDE.md             # Agent context file
  README.md             # Developer documentation
  .npmignore            # Ensures only dist/ + package.json + SKILL.md + LICENSE ship in tarball
```

No `.goreleaser.yml` -- that is Go-specific. The build/release flow uses `npm run build` and `npm pack` instead.

### 3. package.json

```json
{
  "name": "@panthrocorp/aws-s3",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents",
  "main": "dist/index.cjs",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist/",
    "SKILL.md",
    "LICENSE"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "node esbuild.config.mjs",
    "test": "node --test test/exports.test.mjs",
    "prepack": "npm run build"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@aws-sdk/client-s3": "^3.750.0",
    "esbuild": "^0.25.0"
  },
  "license": "MIT-0",
  "author": "panthrocorp",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills",
    "directory": "aws-s3"
  }
}
```

Key decisions:
- `@aws-sdk/client-s3` is a **devDependency** because it gets bundled into `dist/index.cjs` at build time. The published tarball has zero runtime dependencies.
- `main` points to `dist/index.cjs` (CommonJS) because the gateway container uses `require()`.
- The `files` array controls what `npm pack` includes. No `.npmignore` needed if `files` is specified (they are mutually exclusive; `files` is a whitelist and is cleaner).
- `node --test` is the built-in Node.js test runner (available since Node 18), avoiding a test framework dependency.

### 4. esbuild Configuration (esbuild.config.mjs)

```js
import { build } from "esbuild";

await build({
  entryPoints: ["src/index.mjs"],
  bundle: true,
  platform: "node",
  target: "node18",
  format: "cjs",
  outfile: "dist/index.cjs",
  minify: false,        // Keep readable for debugging in gateway
  sourcemap: false,     // Not needed for SDK re-export
  legalComments: "eof", // Preserve license comments at end of file
});
```

Key decisions:
- `platform: "node"` tells esbuild to treat Node built-ins (`http`, `https`, `stream`, etc.) as external -- they are not bundled. This is critical because the AWS SDK uses them.
- `format: "cjs"` because the gateway uses `require()`.
- `target: "node18"` -- conservative minimum, matching typical container base images.
- `minify: false` -- the bundle is not shipped to browsers; readability in the container aids debugging. The size difference is negligible for a server-side package.

### 5. Source Entry Point (src/index.mjs)

```js
export {
  S3Client,
  ListObjectsV2Command,
  GetObjectCommand,
  PutObjectCommand,
  DeleteObjectCommand,
  HeadObjectCommand,
  ListBucketsCommand,
  CopyObjectCommand,
  CreateMultipartUploadCommand,
  UploadPartCommand,
  CompleteMultipartUploadCommand,
  AbortMultipartUploadCommand,
  GetBucketLocationCommand,
} from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";
```

This re-exports all commonly needed symbols. The Linear ticket calls out `S3Client`, `ListObjectsV2Command`, and `GetObjectCommand` as immediate requirements, but including the other common commands costs nothing (they are all in the same package) and avoids a breaking change when the next consumer needs `PutObjectCommand`.

Alternatively, the simplest approach is a full barrel re-export:

```js
export * from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";
```

This is cleaner and future-proof, though the bundle will be slightly larger. Recommend the wildcard re-export for v1 since the size difference is minimal for a server-side package.

### 6. SKILL.md

```yaml
---
name: AWS S3
description: Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents
version: 0.1.0
author: panthrocorp
license: MIT-0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    requires:
      npm:
        - "@panthrocorp/aws-s3"
    emoji: "\U0001FAA3"
    homepage: https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills
    os: ["linux"]
---

# AWS S3 Skill

Pre-bundled AWS S3 SDK for use inside the OpenClaw gateway container. All transitive
dependencies are bundled -- no internet access required at install time.

## Installation

Install from the release tarball into the gateway container:

\```bash
TAG=$(curl -fsSL "https://api.github.com/repos/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases" \
  | grep -o '"tag_name":"aws-s3/v[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
VERSION=${TAG#aws-s3/v}
URL="https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases/download/${TAG}/panthrocorp-aws-s3-${VERSION}.tgz"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install "$URL" --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
\```

Or from a downloaded tarball:

\```bash
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install /path/to/panthrocorp-aws-s3-0.1.0.tgz --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
\```

## Usage

\```js
const { S3Client, ListObjectsV2Command, GetObjectCommand } = require("@panthrocorp/aws-s3");

const client = new S3Client({});  // uses default credential provider chain (IMDS)

const list = await client.send(new ListObjectsV2Command({ Bucket: "my-bucket" }));
const obj = await client.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: "my-bucket", Key: "file.txt" }));
\```

## Authentication

Credentials are resolved via the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. In an EC2/ECS
environment, this uses IMDS (Instance Metadata Service) automatically. No environment
variables or credential files are required.

## Important

- This package bundles @aws-sdk/client-s3 with all transitive dependencies.
- No network access is needed at install time.
- The gateway container must have Node.js available.
```

Notable differences from the Go skills' SKILL.md:
- `requires.npm` instead of `requires.bins` -- this is a new metadata key indicating the skill is an npm package rather than a binary.
- No `requires.env` -- credentials come from IMDS, not environment variables.
- The installation section uses `npm install` with a tarball URL instead of `tar -xz`.

**Gotcha**: The `requires.npm` field is new and may not be recognized by existing clawhub/openclaw tooling. This should be validated with the clawhub team. If `requires.npm` is not supported, use a `requires.files` or similar field, or document the install command in the markdown body only and omit machine-readable npm metadata for now.

### 7. Test Strategy (test/exports.test.mjs)

```js
import { describe, it } from "node:test";
import { strict as assert } from "node:assert";

describe("aws-s3 bundle exports", () => {
  it("exports S3Client", async () => {
    const mod = await import("../dist/index.cjs");
    assert.equal(typeof mod.S3Client, "function");
  });

  it("exports ListObjectsV2Command", async () => {
    const mod = await import("../dist/index.cjs");
    assert.equal(typeof mod.ListObjectsV2Command, "function");
  });

  it("exports GetObjectCommand", async () => {
    const mod = await import("../dist/index.cjs");
    assert.equal(typeof mod.GetObjectCommand, "function");
  });

  it("S3Client can be instantiated", async () => {
    const mod = await import("../dist/index.cjs");
    // Should not throw -- does not make network calls until .send()
    const client = new mod.S3Client({ region: "us-east-1" });
    assert.ok(client);
    client.destroy();
  });
});
```

Tests run against the **built** bundle (`dist/index.cjs`), not the source. This validates that esbuild produced a working artifact. The `npm test` script requires `npm run build` first; CI will run them sequentially.

### 8. CI Changes (.github/workflows/ci.yml)

Add a new job `build-npm` alongside the existing Go `build` job. Update `gate` to depend on both.

New job to add:

```yaml
  build-npm:
    name: Build & Test (npm)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: aws-s3
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"

      - name: Check for skill changes
        id: changes
        run: |
          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
          PATTERN='^aws-s3/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
          if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
            echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Install dependencies
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm run build

      - name: Test
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm test

      - name: Pack (dry-run)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm pack --dry-run

      - name: Skip (no skill changes)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
        run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"
```

Update the `gate` job:

```yaml
  gate:
    name: gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build, build-npm]
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Check build result
        run: |
          for result in "${{ needs.build.result }}" "${{ needs.build-npm.result }}"; do
            if [ "$result" = "failure" ] || [ "$result" = "cancelled" ]; then
              echo "A build failed or was cancelled"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
          echo "All checks passed"
```

### 9. Release Pipeline Changes

#### .release-please-config.json

Add a new entry in `packages`:

```json
"aws-s3": {
  "release-type": "node",
  "component": "aws-s3",
  "changelog-path": "CHANGELOG.md",
  "extra-files": [
    {
      "type": "yaml",
      "path": "SKILL.md",
      "jsonpath": "$.version"
    }
  ]
}
```

`release-type: "node"` tells release-please to read/bump the version from `package.json` (not a Go module). It will also update `package.json` version on release PRs and update `package-lock.json` if present.

#### .release-please-manifest.json

Add the initial version:

```json
{
  "google-workspace": "0.5.0",
  "zoho-mail": "0.3.0",
  "aws-s3": "0.1.0"
}
```

#### .github/workflows/release-please.yml

Add outputs and a new publish job.

New outputs in the `release-please` job:

```yaml
      s3_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--release_created'] }}
      s3_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--tag_name'] }}
```

New publish job:

```yaml
  publish-aws-s3:
    name: Publish aws-s3
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.s3_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: aws-s3
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Test before release
        run: npm run build && npm test

      - name: Build and pack
        id: pack
        run: |
          npm run build
          TARBALL=$(npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename')
          echo "tarball=$TARBALL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Upload tarball to GitHub release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${TAG#aws-s3/v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
```

Key differences from Go publish jobs:
- No GoReleaser step. Replaced by `npm run build && npm pack`.
- No local version tag creation (Go skills need this because GoReleaser expects a simple `vX.Y.Z` tag). The release-please tag `aws-s3/v0.1.0` is sufficient.
- The release artifact is a single `.tgz` tarball (output of `npm pack`) instead of multiple platform-specific `tar.gz` files.
- `npm pack --json` returns structured JSON with the filename, making it easy to capture the tarball name.

### 10. Dependabot Changes (.github/dependabot.yml)

Add an npm ecosystem entry:

```yaml
  - package-ecosystem: npm
    directory: /aws-s3
    schedule:
      interval: weekly
    commit-message:
      prefix: "fix"
    labels:
      - dependencies
    open-pull-requests-limit: 5
```

### 11. .gitignore

Add an `aws-s3/.gitignore` or add to a root `.gitignore`:

```
aws-s3/node_modules/
aws-s3/dist/
```

The `dist/` directory is a build artifact and should not be committed. It is regenerated by `npm run build` in CI and during `npm pack` (via the `prepack` script).

### 12. CLAUDE.md for aws-s3

```markdown
# aws-s3

## Overview

Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents. Unlike the Go-based skills,
this is an npm package that bundles @aws-sdk/client-s3 with all transitive dependencies
into a single CommonJS file.

## Architecture

- `src/index.mjs` -- barrel re-export of @aws-sdk/client-s3 symbols
- `esbuild.config.mjs` -- bundles src/index.mjs into dist/index.cjs (CommonJS, Node 18+)
- `dist/index.cjs` -- (built, gitignored) the complete bundled SDK
- `test/exports.test.mjs` -- validates the bundle exports expected symbols

## Build

npm ci && npm run build

## Test

npm test  (requires build first)

## How it works

The gateway container has Node.js but no internet and no @aws-sdk installed.
This package bundles @aws-sdk/client-s3 and all its transitive dependencies
into a single CJS file using esbuild. The tarball from `npm pack` is installed
into the container via `npm install <tarball>`.

## Credentials

Uses the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. On EC2/ECS this resolves
via IMDS automatically. No environment variables are required.
```

### 13. Gotchas and Edge Cases

1. **`requires.npm` in SKILL.md is a new convention.** The existing skills use `requires.bins`. Confirm with the clawhub/openclaw team that `requires.npm` is a recognized field, or that unrecognized fields are ignored gracefully. If not supported, omit the machine-readable field and rely on the markdown install instructions.

2. **esbuild and Node built-ins.** With `platform: "node"`, esbuild marks Node built-ins as external. This is correct. However, if `@aws-sdk/client-s3` has any optional native dependencies (it should not, but verify), those would fail to bundle. The AWS SDK v3 is pure JavaScript, so this should not be an issue.

3. **Bundle size.** `@aws-sdk/client-s3` v3 is modular, but the full client with credential providers pulls in a significant dependency tree. Expect the bundle to be 1-3 MB. This is acceptable for a server-side package installed once into a container.

4. **`npm pack` tarball naming.** The default name is `panthrocorp-aws-s3-0.1.0.tgz` (npm lowercases and replaces `@`/`/` with the org prefix pattern). The `npm pack --json` approach in the release workflow handles this dynamically.

5. **`npm ci` requires `package-lock.json`.** The `aws-s3/` directory must commit a `package-lock.json`. Without it, `npm ci` fails. Generate it by running `npm install` during initial setup and commit it.

6. **release-please `node` release-type.** This release type bumps `version` in `package.json` and `package-lock.json` automatically. The `extra-files` YAML updater for `SKILL.md` handles the manifest version separately. Both will be updated in the release PR.

7. **No `prepack` in CI.** The `prepack` script in `package.json` runs `npm run build` before `npm pack`. In the release workflow, `npm run build` is called explicitly before `npm pack`, so the build runs twice. This is harmless (idempotent) but could be optimized by removing the explicit build call and relying on `prepack`. However, keeping the explicit call is clearer for CI readability.

8. **Credential provider bundling.** The default credential provider chain (`@aws-sdk/credential-provider-node`) is pulled in transitively by `S3Client`. This includes IMDS, ECS container credentials, environment variables, and shared credentials file providers. All of these are pure JS and will bundle correctly. Verify after the first build that instantiating `new S3Client({})` does not throw import errors.

9. **Container Node.js version.** The esbuild `target: "node18"` should match or be lower than the gateway container's Node version. If the gateway runs Node 20+, `node18` is safe. If it runs Node 16, adjust the target.

10. **Renaming the existing Go `build` job.** For clarity, consider renaming it to `build-go` when adding `build-npm`. This is optional but improves readability. The `gate` job's `needs` array would change to `[build-go, build-npm]`.

### 14. Implementation Sequence

1. Create `aws-s3/` directory with `package.json`, `src/index.mjs`, `esbuild.config.mjs`, `test/exports.test.mjs`
2. Run `npm install` to generate `package-lock.json`
3. Run `npm run build` and `npm test` locally to validate
4. Add `SKILL.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `LICENSE`, `README.md`, `.gitignore`
5. Update `.release-please-config.json` (add `aws-s3` package entry)
6. Update `.release-please-manifest.json` (add `"aws-s3": "0.1.0"`)
7. Update `.github/dependabot.yml` (add npm entry)
8. Update `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (add `build-npm` job, update `gate`)
9. Update `.github/workflows/release-please.yml` (add outputs + `publish-aws-s3` job)
10. Update root `CLAUDE.md` to mention npm-based skills as a second pattern
11. Open PR, verify CI passes (the `build-npm` job should detect changes and run)

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/.release-please-config.json`
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/.github/workflows/release-please.yml`
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/.github/dependabot.yml`
- `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/google-workspace/SKILL.md` (reference for SKILL.md format)</result>
<usage><total_tokens>30404</total_tokens><tool_uses>35</tool_uses><duration_ms>199435</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

input.content:
# PAN-53: aws-s3 npm skill for container scripts

## Context

The OpenClaw gateway container (`ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:2026.3.28`, Node 22) runs `*-email-check` cron jobs that need S3 access. Neither the AWS CLI nor `@aws-sdk/client-s3` is available in the container image. The container has no internet access at runtime.

This skill bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive dependencies into a single CommonJS file, installable via `npm install <tarball>`. It is the first npm-based skill in this repo (existing skills are compiled Go binaries).

First consumer: `discover-agent-emails.mjs` (PAN-52 PR 2), which needs `S3Client`, `ListObjectsV2Command`, and `GetObjectCommand`. Credentials resolve via IMDS (hop limit fix in PAN-52 PR 1).

## 1. New files: `aws-s3/`

```
aws-s3/
  package.json
  package-lock.json       # generated by npm install, committed for CI reproducibility
  src/index.mjs           # barrel re-export: export * from "@aws-sdk/client-s3"
  esbuild.config.mjs      # bundles src/index.mjs -> dist/index.cjs
  test/exports.test.mjs   # validates bundle exports expected symbols
  SKILL.md                # clawhub manifest
  CLAUDE.md               # agent context
  LICENSE                 # MIT-0
```

Build output `dist/` and `node_modules/` are gitignored.

### package.json

```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/aws-s3",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents",
  "main": "dist/index.cjs",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    }
  },
  "files": ["dist/", "SKILL.md", "LICENSE"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "node esbuild.config.mjs",
    "test": "node --test test/exports.test.mjs",
    "prepack": "npm run build"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@aws-sdk/client-s3": "^3.750.0",
    "esbuild": "^0.25.0"
  },
  "license": "MIT-0",
  "author": "panthrocorp",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",

## Context

The OpenClaw gateway container (`ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:2026.3.28`, Node 22) runs `*-email-check` cron jobs that need S3 access. Neither the AWS CLI nor `@aws-sdk/client-s3` is available in the container image. The container has no internet access at runtime.

This skill bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive dependencies into a single CommonJS file, installable via `npm install <tarball>`. It is the first npm-based skill in this repo (existing skills are compiled Go binaries).

First consumer: `discover-agent-emails.mjs` (PAN-52 PR 2), which needs `S3Client`, `ListObjectsV2Command`, and `GetObjectCommand`. Credentials resolve via IMDS (hop limit fix in PAN-52 PR 1).

## 1. New files: `aws-s3/`

aws-s3/
  package.json
  package-lock.json       # generated by npm install, committed for CI reproducibility
  src/index.mjs           # barrel re-export: export * from "@aws-sdk/client-s3"
  esbuild.config.mjs      # bundles src/index.mjs -> dist/index.cjs
  test/exports.test.mjs   # validates bundle exports expected symbols
  SKILL.md                # clawhub manifest
  CLAUDE.md               # agent context
  LICENSE                 # MIT-0
```

Build output `dist/` and `node_modules/` are gitignored.

### package.json

```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/aws-s3",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents",
  "main": "dist/index.cjs",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    }
  },
  "files": ["dist/", "SKILL.md", "LICENSE"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "node esbuild.config.mjs",
    "test": "node --test test/exports.test.mjs",
    "prepack": "npm run build"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@aws-sdk/client-s3": "^3.750.0",
    "esbuild": "^0.25.0"
  },
  "license": "MIT-0",
  "author": "panthrocorp",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",

}

- `S3Client` is exported and can be instantiated

- `GetObjectCommand` is exported (function)

### SKILL.md

```yaml
---
name: AWS S3
description: Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents
version: 0.1.0
author: panthrocorp
license: MIT-0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    requires:
      npm:
        - "@openclaw/aws-s3"

toolUseResult.plan:
# PAN-53: aws-s3 npm skill for container scripts

## Context

The OpenClaw gateway container (`ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:2026.3.28`, Node 22) runs `*-email-check` cron jobs that need S3 access. Neither the AWS CLI nor `@aws-sdk/client-s3` is available in the container image. The container has no internet access at runtime.

This skill bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive dependencies into a single CommonJS file, installable via `npm install <tarball>`. It is the first npm-based skill in this repo (existing skills are compiled Go binaries).

First consumer: `discover-agent-emails.mjs` (PAN-52 PR 2), which needs `S3Client`, `ListObjectsV2Command`, and `GetObjectCommand`. Credentials resolve via IMDS (hop limit fix in PAN-52 PR 1).

## 1. New files: `aws-s3/`

```
aws-s3/
  package.json
  package-lock.json       # generated by npm install, committed for CI reproducibility
  src/index.mjs           # barrel re-export: export * from "@aws-sdk/client-s3"
  esbuild.config.mjs      # bundles src/index.mjs -> dist/index.cjs
  test/exports.test.mjs   # validates bundle exports expected symbols
  SKILL.md                # clawhub manifest
  CLAUDE.md               # agent context
  LICENSE                 # MIT-0
```

Build output `dist/` and `node_modules/` are gitignored.

### package.json

```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/aws-s3",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents",
  "main": "dist/index.cjs",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    }
  },
  "files": ["dist/", "SKILL.md", "LICENSE"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "node esbuild.config.mjs",
    "test": "node --test test/exports.test.mjs",
    "prepack": "npm run build"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@aws-sdk/client-s3": "^3.750.0",
    "esbuild": "^0.25.0"
  },
  "license": "MIT-0",
  "author": "panthrocorp",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills",
    "directory": "aws-s3"
  }
}
```

`@aws-sdk/client-s3` is a devDependency because it is bundled at build time. The published tarball has zero runtime dependencies.

### esbuild.config.mjs

```js
import { build } from "esbuild";

await build({
  entryPoints: ["src/index.mjs"],
  bundle: true,
  platform: "node",
  target: "node22",
  format: "cjs",
  outfile: "dist/index.cjs",
  minify: false,
  sourcemap: false,
  legalComments: "eof",
});
```

`platform: "node"` marks Node built-ins as external. `format: "cjs"` because the gateway uses `require()`. No minification for debuggability.

### src/index.mjs

```js
export * from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";
```

Full barrel re-export. Includes all commands and types from the SDK. The bundle size (~2 MB) is acceptable for a server-side package installed once.

### test/exports.test.mjs

Uses `node:test` (stable in Node 22). Tests run against the built `dist/index.cjs`, not source. Validates:
- `S3Client` is exported and can be instantiated
- `ListObjectsV2Command` is exported (function)
- `GetObjectCommand` is exported (function)

### SKILL.md

```yaml
---
name: AWS S3
description: Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents
version: 0.1.0
author: panthrocorp
license: MIT-0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    requires:
      npm:
        - "@openclaw/aws-s3"
    emoji: "\U0001FAA3"
    homepage: https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills
    os: ["linux"]
---
```

Uses `requires.npm` instead of `requires.bins`. Markdown body documents the install pattern (`docker exec ... npm install <tarball-url>`) and usage examples.

## 2. Modified files

Add to `packages`:

"aws-s3": {
  "release-type": "node",
  "component": "aws-s3",
  "changelog-path": "CHANGELOG.md",
  "extra-files": [
    {
      "type": "yaml",
      "path": "SKILL.md",
      "jsonpath": "$.version"
    }
  ]
}
```

`release-type: "node"` reads/bumps version from `package.json` and `package-lock.json`.

### `.release-please-manifest.json`

Add: `"aws-s3": "0.1.0"`

### `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

input.content:
---
name: AWS S3
description: Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents
version: 0.1.0
author: panthrocorp
license: MIT-0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    requires:
      npm:
        - "@openclaw/aws-s3"
    emoji: "🪣"
    homepage: https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills
    os: ["linux"]
---

# AWS S3 Skill

Pre-bundled AWS S3 SDK for use inside the OpenClaw gateway container. All transitive
dependencies are bundled. No internet access required at install time.

## Installation

Install from the release tarball into the gateway container:

```bash
TAG=$(curl -fsSL "https://api.github.com/repos/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases" \
  | grep -o '"tag_name":"aws-s3/v[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
VERSION=${TAG#aws-s3/v}
URL="https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases/download/${TAG}/openclaw-aws-s3-${VERSION}.tgz"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install "$URL" --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
```

Or from a downloaded tarball:

```bash
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install /path/to/openclaw-aws-s3-0.1.0.tgz \
  --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
```

## Usage

```js
const { S3Client, ListObjectsV2Command, GetObjectCommand } = require("@openclaw/aws-s3");

const client = new S3Client({});

const list = await client.send(new ListObjectsV2Command({
  Bucket: process.env.EMAIL_BUCKET_NAME,
  Prefix: `parsed/${agentName}/`,
}));

const obj = await client.send(new GetObjectCommand({
  Bucket: process.env.EMAIL_BUCKET_NAME,
  Key: "parsed/agent/email.json",
}));
const body = await obj.Body.transformToString();
```

## Authentication

Credentials resolve via the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. In an EC2/ECS
environment this uses IMDS (Instance Metadata Service) automatically. Ensure the IMDS
hop limit is set to 2 or higher when running inside Docker.

No environment variables are required for authentication. `AWS_REGION` should be set
if the region cannot be inferred from instance metadata.

## Important

- This package bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive dependencies.
- No network access is needed at install time.
- The gateway container must have Node.js available.
- All S3 commands from the SDK are available, not just the ones shown above.

input.content:
# aws-s3

## Overview

Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents. Unlike the Go-based skills in this
repo, this is an npm package that bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive
dependencies into a single CommonJS file using esbuild.

## Architecture

- `src/index.mjs` barrel re-exports everything from `@aws-sdk/client-s3`
- `esbuild.config.mjs` bundles `src/index.mjs` into `dist/index.cjs` (CommonJS, Node 22 target)
- `dist/index.cjs` (built, gitignored) is the complete bundled SDK
- `test/exports.test.mjs` validates the bundle exports expected symbols using `node:test`

## Build

```bash
npm ci && npm run build
```

## Test

```bash
npm test
```

Tests require a prior build as they validate the `dist/index.cjs` output.

## How it works

The gateway container has Node.js but no internet and no `@aws-sdk` installed. This package
bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and all its transitive dependencies into a single CJS file
using esbuild. The tarball from `npm pack` is installed into the container via
`npm install <tarball>`.

## Credentials

Uses the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. On EC2/ECS this resolves via IMDS
automatically. No environment variables are required for authentication. The IMDS hop
limit must be 2+ when running inside Docker (handled by PAN-52).

## Release

Release-please uses `release-type: "node"` to version this package. On release, `npm pack`
produces a tarball that is uploaded to the GitHub release and published to clawhub.

build-npm:
    name: Build & Test (npm)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: aws-s3
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"

      - name: Check for skill changes
        id: changes
        run: |
          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
          PATTERN='^aws-s3/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
          if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
            echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Install dependencies
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm run build

      - name: Test
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm test

      - name: Pack (dry-run)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm pack --dry-run

      - name: Skip (no skill changes)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
        run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"

  gate:
    name: gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build, build-npm]
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Check build results
        run: |
          for result in "${{ needs.build.result }}" "${{ needs.build-npm.result }}"; do
            if [ "$result" = "failure" ] || [ "$result" = "cancelled" ]; then
              echo "A build failed or was cancelled"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
          echo "All checks passed"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: >-
      contains(join(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, ','), '') ||
      github.event.pull_request.changed_files == 0 ||
      true
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: google-workspace
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version-file: google-workspace/go.mod

      - name: Check for skill changes
        id: changes
        run: |
          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
          PATTERN='^google-workspace/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
          if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
            echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Verify dependencies
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: go mod verify

      - name: Vet
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: go vet ./...

      - name: Test
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: go test -race -count=1 ./...

      - name: Build
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o /dev/null .

      - name: Skip (no skill changes)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
        run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"

  gate:
    name: gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build]
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Check build result
        run: |
          if [ "${{ needs.build.result }}" = "failure" ] || [ "${{ needs.build.result }}" = "cancelled" ]; then
            echo "Build failed or was cancelled"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "All checks passed"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  release-please:
    name: Release Please
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      releases_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs.releases_created }}
      paths_released: ${{ steps.release.outputs.paths_released }}
      gw_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['google-workspace--release_created'] }}
      gw_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['google-workspace--tag_name'] }}
      zm_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--release_created'] }}
      zm_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--tag_name'] }}
    steps:
      - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
        id: release
        with:
          config-file: .release-please-config.json
          manifest-file: .release-please-manifest.json

  publish-google-workspace:
    name: Publish google-workspace
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.gw_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: google-workspace
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.gw_tag_name }}

      - name: Create local version tag
        id: version
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.gw_tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${TAG#google-workspace/}"
          git tag "$VERSION"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version-file: google-workspace/go.mod

      - name: Test before release
        run: go test -race -count=1 ./...

      - name: Run GoReleaser
        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7
        with:
          args: release --clean
          workdir: google-workspace
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

      - name: Upload assets to release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.gw_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" dist/*.tar.gz dist/checksums.txt
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          SEMVER="${VERSION#v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-google-workspace --version "$SEMVER"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

  publish-zoho-mail:
    name: Publish zoho-mail
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.zm_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: zoho-mail
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}

      - name: Create local version tag
        id: version
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${TAG#zoho-mail/}"
          git tag "$VERSION"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version-file: zoho-mail/go.mod

      - name: Test before release
        run: go test -race -count=1 ./...

      - name: Run GoReleaser
        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7
        with:
          args: release --clean
          workdir: zoho-mail
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

      - name: Upload assets to release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" dist/*.tar.gz dist/checksums.txt
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          SEMVER="${VERSION#v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-zoho-mail --version "$SEMVER"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

79	Release-please updates the `version` field in each skill's `SKILL.md` via a `type: yaml` updater with `jsonpath: $.version` configured in `extra-files`. The `clawhub publish` step passes `--version` explicitly from the release tag rather than relying on clawhub's YAML parser (which cannot handle inline comments or quoted values in frontmatter).
80

82	
83	Release-please bot PRs must be merged manually. GitHub does not trigger `pull_request` workflows for events created by other GitHub Actions, so the auto-merge workflow (`release-please-auto-merge.yml`) does not fire on bot-opened PRs. Dependabot auto-merge works because Dependabot is a first-party GitHub feature.
84	
85	## Adding a new skill
86	
87	1. Create a new directory at the repo root with its own `go.mod`, `main.go`, `cmd/`, and `internal/` packages.
88	2. Add a `SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter (see `google-workspace/SKILL.md` for the schema).

93	
94	## Git conventions
95	
96	- Never commit directly to `main`. Use feature branches and PRs.
97	- Commit messages: `fix:`, `feat:`, or `breaking:` prefix.
98	- Subject line under 50 characters, body uses bullet points with emojis.
99	- Branch protection requires the `gate` CI check to pass and signed commits.
100	- Dependabot PRs are auto-approved and squash-merged.

file.content:
Release-please updates the `version` field in each skill's `SKILL.md` via a `type: yaml` updater with `jsonpath: $.version` configured in `extra-files`. The `clawhub publish` step passes `--version` explicitly from the release tag rather than relying on clawhub's YAML parser (which cannot handle inline comments or quoted values in frontmatter).

### Release-please PR merging

Release-please bot PRs must be merged manually. GitHub does not trigger `pull_request` workflows for events created by other GitHub Actions, so the auto-merge workflow (`release-please-auto-merge.yml`) does not fire on bot-opened PRs. Dependabot auto-merge works because Dependabot is a first-party GitHub feature.

## Adding a new skill

1. Create a new directory at the repo root with its own `go.mod`, `main.go`, `cmd/`, and `internal/` packages.
2. Add a `SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter (see `google-workspace/SKILL.md` for the schema).
3. Add a `.goreleaser.yml` for cross-compilation.
4. Include an MIT-0 `LICENSE` file (clawhub requirement).
5. Add a `gomod` entry for the new skill directory in `.github/dependabot.yml`.
6. Add the new skill path to `.release-please-config.json` and `.release-please-manifest.json`.

## Git conventions

- Never commit directly to `main`. Use feature branches and PRs.
- Commit messages: `fix:`, `feat:`, or `breaking:` prefix.
- Subject line under 50 characters, body uses bullet points with emojis.
- Branch protection requires the `gate` CI check to pass and signed commits.
- Dependabot PRs are auto-approved and squash-merged.

# openclaw-skills

![Language](https://img.shields.io/badge/language-Go-00ADD8?logo=go&logoColor=white)
![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT--0-green)
![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-OpenClaw-blueviolet)

Custom skills for [OpenClaw](https://openclaw.ai) agents, published to the [Claw Skills](https://clawskills.sh) registry.

## Skills

| Skill | Description | Status |
|-------|-------------|--------|
| [google-workspace](./google-workspace/) | Gmail, Calendar, Contacts, Drive (with comments), Docs, and Sheets | Published (`panthrocorp-google-workspace`) |
| [zoho-mail](./zoho-mail/) | Full read/write Zoho Mail access (EU data centre) | Published (`panthrocorp-zoho-mail`) |

## Installation

Install any skill via clawhub:

```bash
clawhub install panthrocorp-google-workspace
clawhub install panthrocorp-zoho-mail
```

## Security

Every skill in this repository is designed with a hostile-instance threat model in mind:

- OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with HKDF-SHA256 key derivation. No unencrypted fallback exists.
- Gmail and Contacts access is strictly read-only with no write code paths.
- Calendar, Drive (comments), Docs, and Sheets write operations are opt-in and gated at both config and runtime level.
- Zoho Mail provides full read/write access, scoped to a dedicated agent mailbox.
- Google Cloud and Zoho API projects are scoped to only the required APIs.

See each skill's README and [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) for the full security policy.

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on branching, commits, and adding new skills.

## Licence

This repository is released under the [MIT No Attribution](./LICENSE) licence.

## Adding a new skill

### Go-based skills

1. Create a new directory at the repo root with its own `go.mod`, `main.go`, `cmd/`, and `internal/` packages.
2. Add a `SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter (see `google-workspace/SKILL.md` for the schema).
3. Add a `.goreleaser.yml` for cross-compilation.
4. Include an MIT-0 `LICENSE` file (clawhub requirement).
5. Add a `gomod` entry for the new skill directory in `.github/dependabot.yml`.
6. Add the new skill path to `.release-please-config.json` (with `release-type: "go"`) and `.release-please-manifest.json`.

### npm-based skills

1. Create a new directory at the repo root with `package.json`, source files, and an esbuild config.
2. Add a `SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter (see `aws-s3/SKILL.md` for the schema). Use `requires.npm` instead of `requires.bins`.
3. Include an MIT-0 `LICENSE` file (clawhub requirement).
4. Add an `npm` entry for the new skill directory in `.github/dependabot.yml`.
5. Add the new skill path to `.release-please-config.json` (with `release-type: "node"`) and `.release-please-manifest.json`.
6. Add a `build-npm` style CI job and a publish job in the release workflow.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project

This repository contains custom OpenClaw skills published to the Claw Skills registry (`clawskills.sh`) under the `panthrocorp` author. Each skill lives in its own subdirectory with an independent Go module, build pipeline, and SKILL.md manifest.

## Repository structure

Each skill is a self-contained directory:

```
<skill-name>/
  SKILL.md              # clawhub manifest (YAML frontmatter + agent instructions)
  LICENSE               # MIT-0 (clawhub requirement)
  go.mod / go.sum       # Go module
  main.go               # entrypoint
  cmd/                  # Cobra CLI commands
  internal/             # internal packages
  .goreleaser.yml       # cross-compilation config
```

## Build and test

From any skill directory:

```bash
go build -o <skill-name> .
go test ./...
```

Cross-compile for the OpenClaw EC2 target (Graviton ARM):

```bash
GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o <skill-name> .
```

## Architecture

Each skill follows a layered pattern:

- `cmd/` uses Cobra for CLI subcommands. Each service (gmail, calendar, contacts, drive, docs, sheets) has its own file registering commands onto `rootCmd`. Helper functions like `gmailClient()` handle config loading, token decryption, and API client creation inline.
- `internal/config/` holds a JSON config model (`config.json`) that gates which services are active and at what access level. A unified `ServiceMode` type (`off`/`readonly`/`readwrite`) is used by Calendar, Drive, Docs, and Sheets. Gmail and Contacts are simple on/off booleans. Config drives OAuth scope selection via `Config.OAuthScopes()`.
- `internal/oauth/` handles the OAuth2 Desktop flow (localhost redirect) and encrypted token storage. Tokens are stored as `token.enc` (AES-256-GCM encrypted JSON). `SaveToken` refuses to write without an encryption key; there is no unencrypted fallback.
- `internal/crypto/` implements AES-256-GCM with HKDF-SHA256 key derivation. Wire format: `salt (16B) || nonce (12B) || ciphertext+tag`.
- `internal/google/` contains thin typed wrappers around Google API services. Each wrapper exposes only the operations the skill permits (e.g. `GmailClient` has no send/modify/delete methods).

Config and credential directory default: `~/.openclaw/credentials/google-workspace/` (overridable via `GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CONFIG_DIR` or `--config-dir`).

## Required environment variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY` | Passphrase for AES-256-GCM token encryption |
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` | Google OAuth2 client ID |
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` | Google OAuth2 client secret |

## Security principles

All skills in this repo follow these rules:

- Read-only operations are the default. Write operations are always opt-in via config.
- OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). No unencrypted fallback exists.
- OAuth scopes are hardcoded per service. Code paths that could escalate scope do not exist.
- Google Cloud projects backing each skill should only enable the APIs the skill uses.
- Every skill must pass clawhub moderation and VirusTotal scanning before publish.

## Publishing

Releases are automated via [release-please](https://github.com/googleapis/release-please). On each merge to `main`, release-please opens or updates a release PR per skill with a generated changelog. Merging that PR creates a GitHub release, tag, and triggers GoReleaser + `clawhub publish`.

Tags are scoped per skill: `google-workspace/v0.1.0`, not `v0.1.0`. GoReleaser config lives inside each skill directory.

Configuration lives in `.release-please-config.json` (package definitions) and `.release-please-manifest.json` (current versions).

### SKILL.md version management

Release-please updates the `version` field in each skill's `SKILL.md` via a `type: yaml` updater with `jsonpath: $.version` configured in `extra-files`. The `clawhub publish` step passes `--version` explicitly from the release tag rather than relying on clawhub's YAML parser (which cannot handle inline comments or quoted values in frontmatter).

### Release-please PR merging

Release-please bot PRs must be merged manually. GitHub does not trigger `pull_request` workflows for events created by other GitHub Actions, so the auto-merge workflow (`release-please-auto-merge.yml`) does not fire on bot-opened PRs. Dependabot auto-merge works because Dependabot is a first-party GitHub feature.

## Adding a new skill

1. Create a new directory at the repo root with its own `go.mod`, `main.go`, `cmd/`, and `internal/` packages.
2. Add a `SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter (see `google-workspace/SKILL.md` for the schema).
3. Add a `.goreleaser.yml` for cross-compilation.
4. Include an MIT-0 `LICENSE` file (clawhub requirement).
5. Add a `gomod` entry for the new skill directory in `.github/dependabot.yml`.
6. Add the new skill path to `.release-please-config.json` and `.release-please-manifest.json`.

## Git conventions

- Never commit directly to `main`. Use feature branches and PRs.
- Commit messages: `fix:`, `feat:`, or `breaking:` prefix.
- Subject line under 50 characters, body uses bullet points with emojis.
- Branch protection requires the `gate` CI check to pass and signed commits.
- Dependabot PRs are auto-approved and squash-merged.

# openclaw-skills

![Language](https://img.shields.io/badge/language-Go-00ADD8?logo=go&logoColor=white)
![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT--0-green)
![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-OpenClaw-blueviolet)

Custom skills for [OpenClaw](https://openclaw.ai) agents, published to the [Claw Skills](https://clawskills.sh) registry.

## Skills

| Skill | Description | Status |
|-------|-------------|--------|
| [google-workspace](./google-workspace/) | Gmail, Calendar, Contacts, Drive (with comments), Docs, and Sheets | Published (`panthrocorp-google-workspace`) |
| [zoho-mail](./zoho-mail/) | Full read/write Zoho Mail access (EU data centre) | Published (`panthrocorp-zoho-mail`) |

## Installation

Install any skill via clawhub:

```bash
clawhub install panthrocorp-google-workspace
clawhub install panthrocorp-zoho-mail
```

## Security

Every skill in this repository is designed with a hostile-instance threat model in mind:

- OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with HKDF-SHA256 key derivation. No unencrypted fallback exists.
- Gmail and Contacts access is strictly read-only with no write code paths.
- Calendar, Drive (comments), Docs, and Sheets write operations are opt-in and gated at both config and runtime level.
- Zoho Mail provides full read/write access, scoped to a dedicated agent mailbox.
- Google Cloud and Zoho API projects are scoped to only the required APIs.

See each skill's README and [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) for the full security policy.

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on branching, commits, and adding new skills.

## Licence

This repository is released under the [MIT No Attribution](./LICENSE) licence.

content.text:
All tasks complete. Here's a summary of what was created and modified:

**New files** (`aws-s3/`):
- `package.json` with `@openclaw/aws-s3` scoped package, esbuild + `@aws-sdk/client-s3` as devDeps
- `src/index.mjs` barrel re-export of `@aws-sdk/client-s3`
- `esbuild.config.mjs` bundling to CJS, Node 22 target
- `test/exports.test.mjs` using `node:test` with `createRequire` (CJS interop)
- `SKILL.md` with `requires.npm` metadata
- `CLAUDE.md`, `LICENSE`, `.gitignore`
- `package-lock.json` (generated)

**Modified files**:
- `.release-please-config.json` added `aws-s3` with `release-type: "node"`
- `.release-please-manifest.json` added `"aws-s3": "0.1.0"`
- `.github/dependabot.yml` added npm ecosystem entry
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` added `build-npm` job, updated `gate` to depend on both
- `.github/workflows/release-please.yml` added outputs + `publish-aws-s3` job
- `CLAUDE.md` split "Adding a new skill" into Go and npm subsections
- `README.md` added Node.js badge, aws-s3 to skills table, install command, security note

Build + test verified locally: 4/4 tests pass, `npm pack` produces a 246 kB tarball with the correct 4 files.

Want me to run the review agents before committing, or shall we proceed?

message.content: yes run review agents

input.description: Get full diff for review agents

diff --git a/.github/dependabot.yml b/.github/dependabot.yml
index 41cd834..a04ad6d 100644
--- a/.github/dependabot.yml
+++ b/.github/dependabot.yml
@@ -21,6 +21,16 @@ updates:
       - dependencies
     open-pull-requests-limit: 5
 
+  - package-ecosystem: npm
+    directory: /aws-s3
+    schedule:
+      interval: weekly
+    commit-message:
+      prefix: "fix"
+    labels:
+      - dependencies
+    open-pull-requests-limit: 5
+
   - package-ecosystem: github-actions
     directory: /
     schedule:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index 52ac273..c4e34a1 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -55,16 +55,62 @@ jobs:
         if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
         run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"
 
+  build-npm:
+    name: Build & Test (npm)
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    defaults:
+      run:
+        working-directory: aws-s3
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+
+      - name: Check for skill changes
+        id: changes
+        run: |
+          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
+          PATTERN='^aws-s3/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
+          if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
+            echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          else
+            echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Build
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
+        run: npm run build
+
+      - name: Test
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
+        run: npm test
+
+      - name: Pack (dry-run)
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
+        run: npm pack --dry-run
+
+      - name: Skip (no skill changes)
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
+        run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"
+
   gate:
     name: gate
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
-    needs: [build]
+    needs: [build, build-npm]
     if: always()
     steps:
-      - name: Check build result
+      - name: Check build results
         run: |
-          if [ "${{ needs.build.result }}" = "failure" ] || [ "${{ needs.build.result }}" = "cancelled" ]; then
-            echo "Build failed or was cancelled"
-            exit 1
-          fi
+          for result in "${{ needs.build.result }}" "${{ needs.build-npm.result }}"; do
+            if [ "$result" = "failure" ] || [ "$result" = "cancelled" ]; then
+              echo "A build failed or was cancelled"
+              exit 1
+            fi
+          done
           echo "All checks passed"
diff --git a/.github/workflows/release-please.yml b/.github/workflows/release-please.yml
index 6671ab9..fa527ed 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/release-please.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/release-please.yml
@@ -20,6 +20,8 @@ jobs:
       gw_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['google-workspace--tag_name'] }}
       zm_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--release_created'] }}
       zm_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--tag_name'] }}
+      s3_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--release_created'] }}
+      s3_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--tag_name'] }}
     steps:
       - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
         id: release
@@ -136,3 +138,50 @@ jobs:
         env:
           CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
           VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
+
+  publish-aws-s3:
+    name: Publish aws-s3
+    needs: release-please
+    if: needs.release-please.outputs.s3_release_created == 'true'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    defaults:
+      run:
+        working-directory: aws-s3
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
+        with:
+          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Test before release
+        run: npm run build && npm test
+
+      - name: Build and pack
+        id: pack
+        run: |
+          npm run build
+          TARBALL=$(npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename')
+          echo "tarball=$TARBALL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+      - name: Upload tarball to GitHub release
+        run: |
+          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
+          gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"
+        env:
+          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+
+      - name: Publish to clawhub
+        if: success()
+        run: |
+          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
+          VERSION="${TAG#aws-s3/v}"
+          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
+          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"
+        env:
+          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}

=== NEW FILE: aws-s3/CLAUDE.md ===
# aws-s3

## Overview

Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents. Unlike the Go-based skills in this
repo, this is an npm package that bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive
dependencies into a single CommonJS file using esbuild.

## Architecture

- `src/index.mjs` barrel re-exports everything from `@aws-sdk/client-s3`
- `esbuild.config.mjs` bundles `src/index.mjs` into `dist/index.cjs` (CommonJS, Node 22 target)
- `dist/index.cjs` (built, gitignored) is the complete bundled SDK
- `test/exports.test.mjs` validates the bundle exports expected symbols using `node:test`

## Build

```bash
npm ci && npm run build
```

## Test

```bash
npm test
```

Tests require a prior build as they validate the `dist/index.cjs` output.

## How it works

The gateway container has Node.js but no internet and no `@aws-sdk` installed. This package
bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and all its transitive dependencies into a single CJS file
using esbuild. The tarball from `npm pack` is installed into the container via
`npm install <tarball>`.

## Credentials

Uses the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. On EC2/ECS this resolves via IMDS
automatically. No environment variables are required for authentication. The IMDS hop
limit must be 2+ when running inside Docker (handled by PAN-52).

## Release

Release-please uses `release-type: "node"` to version this package. On release, `npm pack`
produces a tarball that is uploaded to the GitHub release and published to clawhub.
